title: AP Computer Science A — Diagnostic Assessment

questions.prompt: What is printed when this code runs?
questions.explanation: substring(3, 6) returns characters at indices 3, 4, and 5 — 'p', 'u', 't' → "put".

guiding_questions.text: What index does the character 'c' have in the string 'computer'?
When you call substring(a, b), is the character at index b included in the result?
Write out the index table for 'computer'. What character is at index 5?

questions.explanation: 7 / 2 performs integer division yielding 3, which is then widened to 3.0 when assigned to a double.

guiding_questions.text: What is the data type of the expression x / y before it is assigned to result?
If you wanted the result to be 3.5, what one change would you make to the code?
What would happen if you changed 'int y = 2' to 'double y = 2.0'?

questions.prompt: What is the value of this expression?  !(true && false) || (false || true)

guiding_questions.text: Walk through each sub-expression step by step, starting inside the parentheses.
What does the ! operator do to a boolean value?
If the left side of || is true, does Java even evaluate the right side? What is the result?

questions.explanation: i takes values 1–5. Only i=2 and i=4 satisfy i%2==0, so count increments twice.

guiding_questions.text: What does the % operator compute? What is 4 % 2?
Trace the loop: write down the value of i and count at the end of each iteration.
Which numbers between 1 and 5 are even (i.e., have no remainder when divided by 2)?

questions.explanation: When i=0: inner loop runs 0 times. When i=1: runs 1 time. When i=2: runs 2 times. Total = 3.

guiding_questions.text: When i=1, what is the condition of the inner loop? How many times does it execute?
Make a table: for each value of i, how many times does sum increment?
When i=0, how many times does the inner loop run? Why?

options.D: Compile error: area() is not static

questions.explanation: The constructor sets width=4, height=5. area() returns 4 * 5 = 20.

guiding_questions.text: What happens inside the constructor when we call new Rectangle(4, 5)?
After the constructor runs, what are the values of width and height?
Why doesn't calling r.area() cause a compile error even though area() isn't static?

questions.prompt: Why are instance variables typically declared private in Java?

options.A: Private variables execute faster than public ones
options.B: To prevent the variable from ever being changed after the object is created
options.C: To enforce encapsulation — controlled access through public accessor/mutator methods
options.D: Java requires all instance variables to be private

questions.explanation: Private enforces encapsulation, not immutability. Setters can still change the value.

guiding_questions.text: If a variable is private, can a method inside the same class access it?
What is the difference between private and final in Java?
If private meant 'can never be changed,' how would a setter method work?

questions.explanation: The loop finds the maximum element. 9 (at index 4) is the largest value.

guiding_questions.text: What is the value of max after the loop processes arr[2]=8?
Does the loop check arr[4]=9? What happens to max at that point?
How many iterations does the loop run? What is arr.length for this array?

questions.explanation: remove(1) removes the element at index 1 (which is 20), leaving [10, 30].

guiding_questions.text: ArrayList has two remove methods — one takes an index, one takes an Object. Which is being called here?
After all three add() calls, what does the list look like? What are the indices of each element?
What index does the element 20 have in the list [10, 20, 30]?

questions.explanation: grid[1] is the second row {4, 5, 6}. grid[1][2] is the third element of that row: 6.

guiding_questions.text: In a 2D array, what does the first index represent? What does the second index represent?
Can you list out all three rows of this grid with their indices?
What is grid[2][1]? How is that different from grid[1][2]?
